what_it_does: Acceptance test file for R2 address-keyed context resolution. It creates temporary stores and suites, then verifies scoring helpers, a budget cap, end-to-end injection of locus-addressed annotations/chats into _chat_context, ancestor proximity, preservation of existing context blocks, keyword-consult fallback, and a standalone Suite.context_at read-face API.
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 and uses a global PASS counter with check/expect_raises helpers.
Creates real Suite/NodeRegistry/FsStore instances against the nodes directory.
Tests deterministic pure helpers: address_tree_distance, named R2 constants, _r2_score for recency/proximity/pin.
Attaches 40 annotations and asserts the capped slice is ≤ R2_BUDGET, drops the lowest-scored item, and keeps the highest-scored item.
End-to-end asserts that _chat_context contains a block headed 'CONTEXT RESOLVED AT YOUR LOCUS' with the locus annotation/chat and without a sibling-address annotation.
Verifies ancestor walk gathers a parent-address item and that the exact-locus item scores higher.
Checks that other _chat_context blocks (modes, verbs, models, inbox, graphs, INDICATING, co-presence) remain intact.
Tests keyword fallback via _retrieve_for_consult and no-locus context construction.
Tests Suite.context_at returning a structured bundle, raising on malformed addresses, and returning an honest empty bundle for unattached registered-form addresses.
standouts: The cap proof is the keystone: it asserts not just bounded length but that the lowest-scored item is absent and the highest-scored survives.
Tests the standalone read-face context_at with explicit 400-raise and deny-all empty behaviors.
Deterministic without calling chat()/consult() — no live model needed.
conventions: Uses COMPANY_TEST_RUN environment flag for inbox-hygiene parity.
Uses string headings such as 'CONTEXT RESOLVED AT YOUR LOCUS' and 'presence modes:' as integration anchors.
Names R2 constants as class attributes (R2_LAMBDA, R2_PROXIMITY_WEIGHT, R2_PIN_WEIGHT, R2_BUDGET).
Cleans up with shutil.rmtree in a try/finally.
concerns: Directly assigns suite2._current_locus = None to test the no-locus path, bypassing the intended API.
Heavy reliance on exact human-readable headings in _chat_context makes the test brittle if wording changes.
Imports parse_ui_address from contracts.ui_info and calls it only to assert it does not raise, which is a weak contract check.
Global PASS counter is not thread-safe and mixes assertion side effects with printing.
notes: A deterministic, model-free acceptance test that treats R2's address-keyed context cap as the keystone and also covers the public context_at read-face.
questions: What are the concrete numeric values of R2_LAMBDA, R2_PROXIMITY_WEIGHT, R2_PIN_WEIGHT, and R2_BUDGET?
How does address_tree_distance handle addresses with different schemes or trailing segments?
What exact methods does _r2_gather call on the store (annotations_at, chats_at, addressed events)?
What does contracts.ui_info.parse_ui_address enforce for a single-segment ui:// address?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to acceptance-test R2 address-keyed context resolution with a cap, preservation, and fallback; the file does exactly that across six parts.
apparent_themes: hard budget capping to prevent context flooding
read-face API with fail-fast and deny-all semantics
intention_signals: Encode the R2 design decision that the operator's current locus becomes the primary retrieval key, with a scored, capped slice injected into chat context, while keeping legacy keyword consult and existing context blocks untouched.
novelty: It makes the absence of the lowest-scored item the proof that the cap works, and it tests a public context_at method with explicit malformed-address raise and honest-empty semantics.
contribution: Provides a deterministic acceptance harness that exercises R2 scoring, budget enforcement, locus-addressed context injection, preservation of existing blocks, keyword fallback, and the context_at read-face, giving a file-local specification of expected R2 behavior.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test for R2 address-keyed context resolution that verifies scoring, budget capping, locus injection, block preservation, keyword fallback, and a Suite.context_at read-face API.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that retrieval keyed by the operator's current locus, with recency/proximity/pin scoring and a hard budget, resolves into chat context without breaking existing blocks or fallback paths.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition; on failure it raises with a FAIL label, otherwise it increments PASS and prints an ok line. It is the basic unit-test primitive used throughout the acceptance script.

symbols.does: Helper that passes only if the supplied callable raises an exception.
symbols.description: expect_raises(label, fn) runs fn() and expects it to throw; if no exception occurs it fails with a 'did not raise' message. It is used to verify that Suite.context_at raises on a malformed address.

symbols.does: Local helper that formats a datetime as an ISO string.
symbols.description: iso(dt) returns dt.isoformat(). It is defined inside the test script to simplify construction of timestamp strings for synthetic R2 scoring items.
